meta.name: mmis-dev-knowledge
meta.purpose: Store reusable MMIS automation engineering knowledge for future skill development.

loginStrategies.title: Use mxlogin.jsp and requests.Session for fast MMIS login
loginStrategies.description: MMIS login can be completed without Playwright by requesting login.jsp, parsing loginform, posting credentials to mxlogin.jsp, following the redirect, and extracting uisessionid plus page state from the start center HTML.
applicable_when: Building MMIS automation that only needs authenticated session bootstrap
Reducing login latency before query or download automation
Reusing one login across multiple MMIS operations
advantages: Easy to cache and reuse cookies/session state
Much faster than step-by-step browser automation
Works well as the baseline entrypoint for MMIS automations
disadvantages: Does not automatically solve downstream Maximo event replay
Still requires parsing MMIS HTML for page state variables
recommended_when: Starting any MMIS automation refactor
You need stable login before attempting deeper protocol work
notes: Cache cookies and state for later reuse.
Parse PAGESEQNUM, UISESSIONID, CSRFTOKEN, and APPID from the loaded page.

loginStrategies.title: Keep browser login only as contingency or UI-validation path
loginStrategies.description: Browser-driven login remains useful for screenshot requests, visible UI verification, and fallback when request-driven login or downstream flows are blocked.
applicable_when: HTTP/session flow cannot complete the job
Need to confirm live UI behavior
The user explicitly needs screenshots
advantages: Closer to real user interaction
Good for visual debugging
disadvantages: More fragile due to UI timing and rendering
Slower
recommended_when: Only when request-driven flow cannot complete the task
notes: Do not default to Playwright for routine MMIS automation.

apiPatterns.title: Maximo UI actions are driven by maximo.jsp event posts
apiPatterns.description: MMIS uses POST /maximo/ui/maximo.jsp with form-encoded event payloads to change app, open query menus, and select saved queries.
applicable_when: Replacing UI clicks with request replay
Reverse engineering MMIS actions
advantages: Allows request-driven reconstruction of UI actions
Provides a path to remove most browser waits
disadvantages: Not a simple REST API
Requires exact session, csrf, page sequence, and referer context
recommended_when: Analyzing a UI action before deciding whether Playwright is necessary
notes: Observed events include changeapp and click.
Payload fields include uisessionid, csrftoken, currentfocus, requesttype, responsetype, pageseqnum, xhrseqnum, and events JSON.

apiPatterns.title: Download URL can be extracted from rendered HTML href
apiPatterns.description: After the target MMIS list view is loaded, the download link usually contains openEncodedURL with an actual download URL using _tbldnld.
applicable_when: Downloading list results after a query is already applied
advantages: Bypasses manual click logic once the page is in the right state
Can often be fetched directly with requests
disadvantages: Still depends on the page already reflecting the desired query state
recommended_when: The page HTML already contains the final download href
notes: Look for openEncodedURL("https://..._tbldnld=results_showlist...").

apiPatterns.title: Refresh start center once before replaying changeapp
apiPatterns.description: A fresh request-driven login leaves start center at an earlier PAGESEQNUM than the browser uses for FavoriteApp changeapp. Refresh start center once, then post the changeapp event with the current PAGESEQNUM and CSRFTOKEN.
applicable_when: Switching from start center to another MMIS app using maximo.jsp events
advantages: Avoids sharedSession rejection during request-driven app switch
Matches observed browser event sequencing
disadvantages: Adds one extra GET before the first changeapp event
recommended_when: Replacing FavoriteApp clicks with HTTP event replay
notes: Do not increment pageseqnum beyond the refreshed page state; send the current page sequence.
Follow the changeapp redirect URL and parse the new app state from the loaded page.

apiPatterns.title: Keep the full download URL including uisessionid
apiPatterns.description: The MMIS download href includes _tbldnld plus an appended uisessionid parameter. Truncating the URL at _tbldnld causes the server to return HTTP 500.
applicable_when: Extracting table download links from MMIS HTML
advantages: Allows direct request-driven file download
Eliminates a hidden cause of generic server errors
disadvantages: Requires more careful URL extraction logic than a narrow regex
recommended_when: Turning MMIS export clicks into direct HTTP downloads
notes: Capture the full href passed into openEncodedURL, not only the _tbldnld segment.

apiPatterns.title: MMIS table filters use setvalue and filterrows events
apiPatterns.description: For grid-filter style MMIS queries, typing into filter boxes is replayed as one or more setvalue events, followed by filterrows on the filter-row target.
applicable_when: Converting MMIS table filter UI interactions into HTTP requests
advantages: Removes the need for Playwright typing and Enter presses
Allows deterministic query execution with fewer requests
disadvantages: Requires exact target IDs for each filter field
recommended_when: The query view already exposes filter-row textboxes and download button in the same table
notes: Observed pattern for B-level open fault notices: setvalue on depot field, then setvalue on level field together with filterrows on m6a7dfd2f_tbod_tfrow-tr.
The filter request reused the same pageseqnum and incremented xhrseqnum.

commonErrors.title: Direct event replay may trigger shared-session rejection
commonErrors.description: Even after successful request-driven login, direct POST requests to maximo.jsp can return a deferred redirect to exit.jsp?sharedSession=1.
applicable_when: Replaying Maximo UI events using requests.Session
advantages: Documents a critical protocol limitation early
disadvantages: Blocks pure HTTP completion of some MMIS workflows
recommended_when: Assessing feasibility of removing browser automation entirely
notes: This was traced to stale start center PAGESEQNUM/CSRFTOKEN during changeapp replay.
Refresh start center once before the first changeapp event.

commonErrors.title: Python Playwright fallback can fail with WinError 5 in the current sandbox
commonErrors.description: Launching Playwright from Python in this environment can fail before browser start with WinError 5 access denied on Windows async subprocess plumbing.
applicable_when: Using Playwright Python as fallback from mmisClient.py inside the current Codex environment
advantages: Explains why MCP Playwright may work while Python Playwright does not
disadvantages: Prevents in-process browser fallback verification here
recommended_when: Choosing between MCP browser tools and Python Playwright fallback
notes: Do not assume MCP Playwright behavior matches Python Playwright behavior in sandboxed environments.

performanceTips.title: Move login to HTTP first for immediate speed gains
performanceTips.description: Replacing login clicks and waits with a single request-driven bootstrap gives the largest early speed improvement.
applicable_when: Optimizing an existing Playwright-heavy MMIS flow
advantages: Allows session reuse
Cuts repeated UI waits
disadvantages: Downstream protocol may still require deeper analysis
recommended_when: Refactoring any existing MMIS skill for speed
notes: Do not wait for the entire flow to be request-driven before adopting request-driven login.

performanceTips.title: Log step timing and retry activity for every MMIS core flow
performanceTips.description: MMIS automation debugging becomes much faster when each major step logs start, end, duration, and retry activity instead of only final success or failure.
applicable_when: Optimizing MMIS client stability
Investigating slow or flaky MMIS automation
advantages: Makes bottlenecks visible immediately
Makes retry behavior observable without manual tracing
disadvantages: Slightly more verbose logging output
recommended_when: Building or refactoring reusable MMIS core clients
notes: Return step metrics together with the final JSON result when practical.
Log request status, duration, and attempt number.

performanceTips.title: Reuse cached session whenever valid
performanceTips.description: Persist cookies and parsed page state so repeated MMIS operations do not start from login each time.
applicable_when: Multiple MMIS automations run in the same work period
advantages: Improves perceived responsiveness
Reduces repeated authentication overhead
disadvantages: Requires validation and expiry handling
recommended_when: Building reusable MMIS client libraries
notes: Validate cached sessions before trusting them.

stableWorkflows.title: Download unprocessed fault notices, then run Excel formatter
stableWorkflows.description: The stable end-to-end workflow is: establish session, enter fault notice management, apply the fixed saved query, download 故障通報管理MMDD.xlsx, then run the dedicated Excel formatter.
applicable_when: Automating 本段未處理通報(車輛配屬段)
advantages: Keeps post-processing deterministic
Separates MMIS acquisition from Excel transformation
disadvantages: Still depends on the saved query name and current MMIS page structure remaining stable
recommended_when: Implementing the current main MMIS automation use case
notes: The MMIS acquisition path is now fully request-driven for login, app switch, query apply, and file download.
Acquisition and formatting should remain decoupled.
Excel formatting is already stable as a separate script.

stableWorkflows.title: Phase 1 Playwright MMIS queries should bootstrap from shared HTTP login
stableWorkflows.description: When a new MMIS query skill is still in browser-driven Phase 1, reuse MMISClient.login() to obtain cookies and session cache first, then inject those cookies into Playwright before navigating to start center.
applicable_when: Building a new MMIS query skill in Playwright before HTTP refactor
advantages: Reuses the validated login path
Keeps browser automation focused on the query-only portion
Makes Phase 2 HTTP migration easier because login is already shared
disadvantages: Still depends on Playwright runtime availability
recommended_when: A new MMIS flow needs browser correctness first but should remain aligned with the long-term HTTP architecture
notes: If injected cookies do not authenticate the browser, only then fall back to visible page login inside Playwright.
Keep wrapper interfaces stable so Phase 2 can swap the implementation without changing the skill entrypoint.

stableWorkflows.title: Open B-level fault notices can run HTTP-first with Playwright fallback
stableWorkflows.description: The 本段未結案 B 級故障通報 flow can be completed through HTTP by reusing the same app-switch and saved-query pattern, then sending setvalue/filterrows events and downloading the resulting _tbldnld link. Keep the validated Playwright script as fallback only.
applicable_when: Automating 故障通報未結案清單 with depot and level filters
advantages: Cuts runtime substantially compared with full browser automation
Preserves a working fallback when MMIS patterns change
disadvantages: Still depends on current field target IDs remaining stable
recommended_when: Implementing the Phase 2 version of the B-level open fault notice skill
notes: Observed runtime was about 1.7 seconds in HTTP mode with session reuse.
Fallback should call the validated Playwright script only after HTTP failure.

stableWorkflows.title: 1A work-order linked fault notices are stable with Playwright plus HTTP login bootstrap
stableWorkflows.description: The MMIS 動力車日檢(1A) linked-fault-notice lookup is stable as a Playwright workflow when the browser session is bootstrapped from MMISClient.login(), then the script waits for the zz_pmwo1a app URL and the query fields before searching the work order. The refactored version removes all screenshot I/O and is structured for repeated subroutine-style calls.
applicable_when: Automating MMIS 動力車日檢(1A) work-order detail lookup
Need to retrieve fault notices linked to one 1A work order
Need browser-only traversal into the work-order detail page
advantages: Handles 0 results, 1 result, multiple linked fault notices, and not-found cases
Reuses the faster shared HTTP login path before opening Playwright
Avoids screenshot overhead and disk I/O in high-frequency calls
Can be reused as a lower-level query helper for future batch workflows
disadvantages: Still depends on Playwright runtime availability
App open remains slower than the existing HTTP-only MMIS list queries
recommended_when: A MMIS workflow must open the 1A detail page and scrape linked notices from the rendered UI
The query will be reused by higher-level MMIS skills and should not perform screenshot side effects
notes: Validated result patterns with live work orders: 115-1A-23391 (3 linked notices), 115-1A-25365 (1 linked notice), 115-1A-22216 (2 linked notices), 115-1A-05159 (0 linked notices), and 115-1A-15159 (work order not found).
Waiting for the zz_pmwo1a URL plus visible query inputs is more reliable than waiting only on networkidle after clicking 動力車日檢(1A).
For high-frequency internal use, prefer clear step logs over screenshots or debug artifacts.
Keep browser, context, and page reusable so future batch lookup can avoid reopening Playwright per work order.

toolingNotes.title: Always read and update this knowledge around MMIS development
toolingNotes.description: New MMIS skills should begin by consulting existing knowledge and end by writing back validated reusable lessons.
applicable_when: Any MMIS skill creation, refactor, or bug fix
advantages: Avoids rediscovering the same protocol and failure patterns
Improves consistency across MMIS skills
disadvantages: Requires discipline to keep knowledge updated
recommended_when: At the start and end of every MMIS automation task
notes: Prefer concise, structured, high-signal updates.

toolingNotes.title: Force UTF-8 when wrapper scripts launch Python MMIS tools
toolingNotes.description: When wrapper scripts invoke MMIS Python tools, set PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 so Chinese logs remain readable in console output.
applicable_when: Launching MMIS client scripts through thin wrapper scripts
advantages: Prevents garbled Traditional Chinese logs
Improves observability during live operations
disadvantages: Requires wrapper maintenance discipline
recommended_when: Any wrapper forwards console output from MMIS Python tools
notes: Apply this in wrapper scripts, not only inside the core client.
